data.narrative:
Akash Systems is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 private peers at the same stage and sector (Cowboy Space Corporation at $2.0B, Aalyria at $1.3B, and Hubble Network at $400M), as well as 5 public Space & Aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) that serve as sector anchors.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $40M raised baseline, implying $76M valuation. The peer-median method establishes a $1.3B implied value based on the three same-stage, same-sector comparables, with a range spanning $400M to $2.0B. The public-comp anchoring approach cannot be applied owing to the absence of target revenue data, a standard constraint for private company analysis.

Synthesizing these methods yields a triangulated range of $65M to $1.5B, with a median of $688M. The wide range reflects material dispersion in private peer valuations and the tension between early-stage fundraising multiples and late-stage comparable valuations. Key uncertainties include actual revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and technology maturity relative to the peer set—variables not available in current documentation.
